task_completion.description: Page 1 displays a title containing 'Circulatory System' and mentions both 'Pulmonary' and 'Systemic', with a subtitle about blood flow or oxygen transport.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Title text visible on the first page of the flipchart.

task_completion.description: Page 2 has three rectangles arranged vertically and labeled 'HEART' (center), 'LUNGS' (above), and 'BODY TISSUES' (below).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three distinct rectangular shapes arranged top-center-bottom with visible text labels.

task_completion.description: Page 2 has blue arrows for deoxygenated blood (Heart to Lungs, Body to Heart) and red arrows for oxygenated blood (Lungs to Heart, Heart to Body), with each arrow labeled with its vessel name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least 4 arrows or lines in two distinct colors (blue and red) connecting the three rectangles. Arrow labels should include vessel names: Pulmonary Artery, Pulmonary Vein, Aorta, Vena Cava.

task_completion.description: Page 2 has green circles labeled 'Gas Exchange' near both LUNGS and BODY TISSUES, and labels 'Pulmonary Loop' and 'Systemic Loop' identifying each circuit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two green circular shapes with Gas Exchange text, plus circuit identification labels.

task_completion.description: Page 3 shows three blood vessel cross-sections side by side. The Artery has a thick colored outer ring with small white center. The Vein has a thin colored outer ring with larger white center. The Capillary is noticeably smaller than the other two.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three circular diagrams arranged horizontally. Artery should have visibly thicker wall than Vein. Capillary should be visibly smaller. Each labeled with vessel name. Artery in red tones, Vein in blue tones.

task_completion.description: Each vessel cross-section on Page 3 has its name and a key structural characteristic described (e.g., thick muscular walls, thin walls with valves, one cell thick).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Descriptive text below or near each vessel diagram.

task_completion.description: Page 4 has a titled table with 4 columns (Vessel, O2 Saturation, Pressure, Direction) and 4 data rows with physiologically accurate values for Pulmonary Artery, Pulmonary Vein, Aorta, and Vena Cava.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A structured grid or table on Page 4 with column headers and 4 data rows containing numeric values for oxygen saturation and blood pressure.

task_completion.description: The flipchart is saved as 'circulatory_system_lesson.flipchart' with 4 pages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Save dialog completed or title bar showing saved filename instead of 'Untitled'.

integrity.description: The agent used the ActivInspire GUI application to create the flipchart content.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the ActivInspire interface (toolbar, canvas, page browser) being actively used throughout the task.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to programmatically generate the flipchart file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing Python scripts, echo commands, or direct file manipulation to create .flipchart content.

integrity.description: Content was genuinely drawn and typed on the ActivInspire canvas using drawing and text tools, not pasted as a pre-made image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Individual shapes, text boxes, and lines are visible as separate canvas objects, showing active use of ActivInspire drawing tools.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected file is a 4-page flipchart saved as 'circulatory_system_lesson.flipchart' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\Flipcharts\'. Page 1: title page with 'The Circulatory System: Pulmonary & Systemic Loops'. Page 2: dual-loop flow diagram with HEART rectangle at center, LUNGS rectangle above, BODY TISSUES rectangle below, connected by blue arrows (deoxygenated: Pulmonary Artery upward, Vena Cava upward) and red arrows (oxygenated: Pulmonary Vein downward, Aorta downward), plus two green Gas Exchange circles and Pulmonary/Systemic Loop labels. Page 3: three side-by-side vessel cross-sections drawn as concentric circles - Artery (thick red wall, small white lumen), Vein (thin blue wall, large white lumen), Capillary (tiny thin circle), each labeled with name and characteristic. Page 4: data table titled 'Blood Vessel Oxygen & Pressure Data' with 4 columns (Vessel, O2%, Pressure mmHg, Direction) and 4 rows: Pulmonary Artery (75, 25, Heart to Lungs), Pulmonary Vein (100, 8, Lungs to Heart), Aorta (98, 120, Heart to Body), Vena Cava (75, 5, Body to Heart).
